title: Revert v47.8 --ipv4/--ipv6-timeout options (crash regression)
description:
Build 144 on TestFlight crashed for the PO on the first channel selection with EXC_BAD_ACCESS / SIGSEGV at libvlc_media_player_new (offset 16, x0=NULL). Crash incident C05C70B4-769E-492F-80B2-F3CFCFDEAF77 on 2026-05-21 07:57:05.

Root cause: the --ipv4-timeout=2000 and --ipv6-timeout=2000 libvlc options I added in v47.8 are not recognized by this MobileVLCKit's bundled libvlc. When VLCMediaPlayer(options:) is constructed with them, libvlc fails to initialize its instance, VLCLibrary's internal p_libvlc_int ends up NULL, and the subsequent libvlc_media_player_new call dereferences NULL and segfaults.

The first VLCMediaPlayer() at AudioPlayerService init() didn't crash because it uses no options (default-config libvlc). Crash happens on the first retirePlayer(options:) call — i.e., the first channel play attempt.

Fix: remove --ipv4-timeout and --ipv6-timeout from both retirePlayer(options:) call sites (startStream and the buffered-playback path). Replace v47.8's option-block comment with a warning that documents these options crashed and should not be re-added without verifying the libvlc version supports them.

This was the exact failure mode the 2026-05-20-18 retro warned about: shipped a defensive change without validating that the libvlc option names were valid for this build. The bd v47.8 close note literally said 'exact knob varies by MobileVLCKit version' — should have verified before shipping.
close_reason: Removed --ipv4-timeout=2000 and --ipv6-timeout=2000 from both retirePlayer(options:) call sites in AudioPlayerService.swift (startStream around line 750, buffered-playback around line 1010). Left a code-comment warning at the startStream site documenting that these options crash libvlc init in this MobileVLCKit version and should not be re-added without verification. Build 145 succeeded; app launches and 52 unit tests pass.

title: Session ownership + integration rewire
PCM pipeline: AVAudioEngine routing + format negotiation + threading
Spike: ObjC bridge to libvlc_audio_set_callbacks, prove amem prevents setActive(false)
description: ObjC bridge importing VLCMediaPlayer+Internal.h (playerInstance) and vlc/libvlc_media_player.h. Add MobileVLCKit PrivateHeaders to HEADER_SEARCH_PATHS in project.yml. Swift wrapper registers no-op play/format callbacks. Call wrapper in startStream() pre-play. Audio silent in this phase. Log every callback firing. Exit: logs show callbacks fire AND otherAudio stays false during channel-change debounce.

title: Phase: Replace VLCKit audio output with app-owned AVAudioEngine pipeline
description:
Root-cause fix for the Apple Music focus grab. VLCKit's iOS audiounit module owns the AVAudioSession and calls setActive(false, .notifyOthersOnDeactivation) on mediaPlayer.stop() (confirmed via VLC 3.0.x source). Workarounds (assertSessionOwnership pre-debounce in build 146) don't prevent it because the deactivation is hardcoded with no opt-out flag.

Real fix: register PCM callbacks via libvlc_audio_set_callbacks(), which switches VLC to in-memory output (amem module). audiounit_ios is never loaded; SessionManager is never invoked; no setActive(false) on stop. AdagioStream owns AVAudioSession exclusively and routes PCM through its own AVAudioEngine.

Requires reaching into VLCKit private API (VLCMediaPlayer+Internal.h playerInstance) via a thin Objective-C bridge in the AdagioStream target, since the framework modulemap excludes the libvlc C headers.

Children:
1. ObjC bridge + spike — prove amem callbacks prevent VLC session manipulation
2. PCM pipeline — AVAudioEngine routing, format negotiation, threading
3. Session ownership + integration — Adagio owns AVAudioSession; rewire interruption / route / NowPlaying / CarPlay paths
4. Cleanup — remove the assertSessionOwnership workarounds layered in earlier

title: Reject failing playlist edits — don't persist broken updates
description:
Companion to mobilemusic-7rr. ProviderManager.updateProvider() replaces the provider in-place and calls saveProviders() unconditionally; if the user edits a working provider to broken credentials, the broken state is persisted before loadChannels() reveals the failure.

Fix: pre-validate the updated provider with loadChannelsWithRetry before replacement. If it throws or returns 0 channels, set error and return without modifying providers — the prior good config stays intact on disk. Only on successful validation do we replace + save + refresh.

Call site is AddProviderView.swift lines 163-174 (editing branch).

title: Reject failing playlist adds — don't persist providers with 0 channels
description:
User report: adding a playlist that fails to load still creates an entry with 0 channels.

Root cause: ProviderManager.addProvider() appends the provider to the in-memory list and calls saveProviders() *before* attempting to load channels. If loadChannels() then fails (bad URL, auth error, network), the provider is already on disk.

Fix: validate by calling loadChannels(from:) (with retry) before persisting. If it throws or returns 0 channels, set the user-facing error and return without appending or saving. Existing call sites in AddProviderView and WelcomeSetupView already check providerManager.error after the call, so the contract is preserved.

title: Fix Apple Music focus grab during channel change
description:
When Apple Music is playing, user opens Adagio and plays a stream (works fine), then changes channels — during the 1.5s debounce window in AudioPlayerService.play(), Apple Music auto-resumes briefly because mediaPlayer.stop() runs immediately but the session-takeover dance (setActive(false, .notifyOthersOnDeactivation) → setActive(true)) is deferred until startStream() runs after the debounce.

Root cause: AudioPlayerService.swift:604 calls mediaPlayer.stop() immediately, but the session takeover at lines 709-712 lives inside startStream(), which is debounced 1.5s when needsDelay is true.

Fix: move the session-takeover dance into play() itself, before the debounce timer is scheduled, so Adagio asserts session ownership before yielding the 1.5s window.

Instrumentation: log isOtherAudioPlaying values at three points — entry to play(), just after mediaPlayer.stop(), and just before startStream() runs — so we can confirm Apple Music sneaks in during the gap if the fix doesn't fully resolve it.

title: Bound VLC network read so poll() can't hang on stalled sockets
description:
Root cause of the watchdog kill is Thread 11 in vlc_tls_Read → vlc_poll_i11e_inner → poll() on a TLS socket that stopped delivering bytes (cell radio stalled during incoming call). poll() had no short timeout, so VLC's input thread sat indefinitely and starved every join up the chain.

Investigate MobileVLCKit options to bound this. Candidates (exact names vary by VLCKit version):
- :http-reconnect (already set per debug log)
- :network-caching=10000 (already set; controls buffer not socket timeout)
- :ipv4-timeout / :ipv6-timeout — connection-time timeouts
- :sout-livehttp-caching — output side, not input
- Lower-level: configure custom VLCMedia options to add :clock-jitter and a tighter input timeout
- Worst case: post-process by adding a watchdog that calls media = nil if no bytes flow for N seconds

Reference the existing zero-byte retry logic (vlcZeroByteRetryCount, line 588) and the Buffering timeout retry at line ~1372 — there's already a 20s no-data timeout but it only fires DURING playback startup, not for an established stream that stalls.

Acceptance: a stalled HLS socket triggers media=nil within ≤2s, freeing VLC's input thread before any teardown is attempted.
close_reason:
Added --ipv4-timeout=2000 and --ipv6-timeout=2000 to both retirePlayer call sites (startStream and the buffered-playback path).

Research findings (recorded inline in startStream): VLC's steady-state poll() inside vlc_tls_Read uses an infinite timeout that cannot be configured via libvlc options. The connect timeouts only bound the initial TCP handshake (defaults to 5s, now 2s). For mid-stream stalls — the actual crash mechanism — the syncState() data-flow watchdog (dataFlowStaleTimeout = 8s, line 75) is the only mechanism, and it already exists.

So v47.8 ships a narrow improvement (faster reconnect on connection-time stalls) and documents the unfixable steady-state case. The combined v47 fix surface is:
- v47.5: timing instrumentation on all 8 VLC teardown call sites
- v47.6: detach media before stop in retirePlayer (input severed before drain)
- v47.7: closure capture audit complete — orphan reference lives inside VLCKit
- v47.8: 2s connect timeouts + documented limit

Build 141 succeeded; 52 unit tests pass.

title: Audit closure captures for orphan VLCMediaPlayer strong references
description:
Find the second strong reference to VLCMediaPlayer that escaped retirePlayer's [old] utility-queue release pattern. Stack trace shows _Block_release on a main-queue block triggered the dealloc, meaning some block on main was holding a reference that outlived the [old] capture.

Audit targets in AudioPlayerService.swift:
1. interruptionFallbackWorkItem (line 288) — DispatchWorkItem retained by asyncAfter, captures self
2. channelNameOverlayWorkItem (line 600) — DispatchWorkItem captures [weak self]
3. The 0.5s post-interruption closure (line 339) — no [weak self], captures self strongly
4. Any other DispatchWorkItem/asyncAfter that touches self.mediaPlayer
5. VLC delegate callbacks — verify VLCKit's delegate is assign/unsafe_unretained (should be) and not retaining the player back
6. Any local 'let p = mediaPlayer' patterns that escape into closures

Grep starting point:
    grep -nE 'mediaPlayer|VLCMediaPlayer' AdagioStream/Services/AudioPlayerService.swift | grep -v '^\s*//'

If a real orphan is found, either capture [weak self] or convert to a dedicated [weak mediaPlayer] capture and guard.
close_reason:
Audit complete. NO orphan VLCMediaPlayer reference found in app code:

1. retirePlayer's [old] capture is the only explicit player capture; dispatched to utility queue.
2. All DispatchWorkItem closures (interruptionFallbackWorkItem line 320, channelNameOverlayWorkItem line 632, debounce workItem line 651) use [weak self].
3. The two interruption.ended asyncAfter closures (lines 371, 408) strong-capture self but access self.mediaPlayer (re-evaluated per call) — so they reach the NEW player after a retirePlayer, not the OLD one.
4. Combine sinks (sxmCancellable line 179, espnCancellable line 190) use [weak self].
5. VLC delegate methods are 'nonisolated' wrappers that dispatch Task { @MainActor in } — no aNotification capture, and self is either weakly captured or accessed via self.mediaPlayer.
6. NotificationCenter observers all target AVAudioSession.sharedInstance(), not the media player.

Conclusion: the orphan reference responsible for the dealloc-on-main in the FRONTBOARD crash most likely lives inside VLCKit's own dispatch internals (VLCKit posts state-change notifications via NSNotification with the player as object; the runloop's pending notification queue can strong-retain the player). That's outside our control.

The right mitigation is v47.6 (detach media before stop, done) + v47.8 (bound the network read so poll() can't hang regardless of who runs the final dealloc).

title: Detach media before stop in retirePlayer (safer teardown order)
description:
In AudioPlayerService.swift retirePlayer() (line 119), the current order is:
    old.stop()       // line 121
    old.media = nil  // line 122
    old.delegate = nil  // line 123

Swap so media is detached BEFORE stop is called:
    old.media = nil  // sever the input source first
    old.delegate = nil
    old.stop()       // now has nothing to drain

Rationale: stop() can synchronously join VLC's input/decoder threads. If the input thread is in vlc_stream_ReadPartial → poll() on a stalled socket, stop() inherits the block. Clearing media first signals the input layer to exit before stop runs.

Low-risk change. Pair with the instrumentation issue to verify the new ordering doesn't introduce a different stall.
close_reason: Swapped order in retirePlayer to detach media (and clear delegate) BEFORE calling stop(). stop() synchronously drains VLC's input/decoder threads, so clearing media first severs the input source. Build 141; 52 tests pass.

title: Add timing instrumentation around VLC player teardown
description:
Add Date()-based timing logs around the VLC teardown call sites in AudioPlayerService.swift so the next watchdog kill leaves an unambiguous fingerprint.

Specifically:
- Wrap mediaPlayer.stop() at line 121 in retirePlayer with before/after timestamps; log elapsed.
- Inside the [old] background release block at line 131, log 'VLC old player released on utility queue' before _ = old.
- Wrap the new VLCMediaPlayer(options:) construction with timing.
- Log when mediaPlayer.stop() is called in stop() (line 1074) and other call sites with elapsed timing.

Goal: if main thread stalls again, we see exactly which call blocked and for how long, even if it doesn't reach 10s.
close_reason: Added timed() helper and wrapped all 5 VLC teardown call sites (retirePlayer stop/media=nil/delegate=nil/new player, play() teardown, pause() stop, stop() stop+media=nil, reduced-buffer giveup stop+media=nil). Also added entry/exit logs in the utility-queue dispose block. Build 141 succeeded; all 52 unit tests pass.

title: VLC teardown blocks main thread → FRONTBOARD scene-update watchdog kill
description:
Confirmed crash from incident on 2026-05-20 at 16:47:12. Termination reason: FRONTBOARD code 0x8BADF00D, scene-update watchdog transgression, 10s wall-clock allowance exceeded while in Background.

Main thread stack: _Block_release on a main-queue block → -[VLCMediaPlayer dealloc] → libvlc_media_player_destroy → pthread_join. VLC internal teardown threads (input_Close, input_DecoderDelete) also in pthread_join. Thread 11 stuck in vlc_stream_ReadPartial → vlc_tls_Read → poll() on a hung TLS socket (HLS .ts segment) — the cellular path stalled mid-stream when the user got an incoming phone call.

retirePlayer() at AudioPlayerService.swift:119 already dispatches the old player release to a utility queue via [old] capture, but a second strong reference to the same VLCMediaPlayer escaped that pattern and the final dealloc landed on main when its block was disposed.

Incident artifacts:
- Crash report: 1C484B9A-8A10-46DB-A48C-22D930B58785 (process 59425, build 140)
- Debug log: ~/adagiostream-debug.log shows 2m 24s of main-thread starvation (16:44:52 → 16:47:16) followed by a clean cold-launch trace at CarPlay reconnect
- User-visible: app died silently during a declined call; CarPlay-only cold launch followed at 16:47:16

Symptoms downstream of this crash (slow first buffer, missing now-playing, dead remote commands) are tracked separately in the cold-launch UX epic.
close_reason: All 4 children closed. Build 141 ships: VLC teardown timing instrumentation, media detached before stop in retirePlayer, 2s TCP connect timeouts, closure capture audit complete. Real-world validation pending: next phone call during CarPlay playback.

title: Enable DebugLogger from CarPlay scene independent of iOS scene
description:
Problem: DebugLogger.shared.isEnabled is only set by SettingsViewModel.loadSettings() (SettingsViewModel.swift:45), which runs only when the iOS WindowGroup body initializes (AdagioStreamApp.swift:26). On a true CarPlay-only cold launch (phone locked, just plugged in), the iOS scene never comes up and DebugLogger stays disabled — so every existing user log shows only iOS+CarPlay launches, not the failing CarPlay-only case.

Fix: In CarPlaySceneDelegate.templateApplicationScene(_:didConnect:), load AppSettings from PersistenceService and set DebugLogger.shared.isEnabled = settings.debugLoggingEnabled before configure() runs (or as early as possible so configure()'s own logs are captured). Keep the SettingsViewModel path intact so iOS toggle changes still propagate.

Done when:
- A CarPlay-only launch produces CARPLAY CONNECTED / configure() starting / PROVIDERS logs in the file.
- Toggling debug logging off in iOS Settings still disables logging in CarPlay-only sessions (no regression).
close_reason: Implemented in CarPlaySceneDelegate.applyDebugLoggingPreference(). Reads AppSettings synchronously from disk and applies debugLoggingEnabled to DebugLogger.shared before the first log call, so CarPlay-only cold launches now capture the failure path. Build v1.1 (140) installed and launched in simulator.

title: Phase 2: Capture and harden CarPlay-only cold launches
description: Phase 1 (3xs) added per-provider retries and a 15s recovery check, but CarPlay-only launches still fail to load XC providers. The recovery code is in place but we have no logs of the failing case because DebugLogger only flips on from SettingsViewModel, which runs only when the iOS scene initializes. Phase 2 captures the failure path and hardens the recovery's empty-providers gap.
close_reason: Both children closed. pid.2 enabled DebugLogger from CarPlay scene (shipped cf39dee). pid.3 closed the empty-providers-list false-positive in the recovery check (this commit).

title: Phase 1: Fix XC providers missing in CarPlay-only launches
description:
When CarPlay launches the app standalone (no iOS UI active), Xtream Codes providers fail silently and contribute 0 channels. Only M3U providers and custom playlists appear. Opening the iOS app at the same time loads everything correctly.

Suspected cause: background-launch network behavior magnified by XC's 3 sequential calls (authenticate -> categories -> streams), with no retry beyond inner fetch() 5xx/decoding retries, and no logging in the catch path.

Goal: instrument the failure path, add per-provider retries, and add a CarPlay-side recovery if XC providers return zero channels on cold launch.

title: Raise default bufferDuration to 5s with one-time migration + buffer-source logging
description: User reported cellular drive was bad on 1.1; logs showed network-caching=2000ms. The 2s default is too small for cellular. Bump Constants.defaultBufferDuration to 5.0 and add a one-time migration in SettingsViewModel.loadSettings (if value == 2.0, bump to 5.0 and persist). Add logging for buffer source on settings load and on updateBufferDuration changes so future log uploads make this visible without inspecting the per-play stream-start line.
close_reason: Bumped Constants.defaultBufferDuration 2->5 with legacy 2.0 migration. Added redacted settings snapshot at load (build, OS, device, settings, provider counts by type, channel count, network path). Logged updateBufferDuration changes and migration source. Reverted audio-time-stretch / http-continuous (kept http-reconnect) and fixed path-monitor log order. Build 138 green, 52/52 tests pass, app launches in simulator.

title: Revert audio-time-stretch / http-continuous after 1.1 cellular regression
description:
User reported HORRIBLE driving experience on 1.1 build 137: lots of skipping, cut outs, and jump aheads. Log shows constant buffer underruns (lostAudio counter climbing steadily) during cellular dips to 3-7 kbps.

Diagnosis:
- audio-time-stretch slows/speeds playback to absorb drift -> pitch artifacts that user perceives as 'skipping' and 'jump aheads'
- http-continuous keeps broken sockets alive, leading to forward-skips when stream resumes
- http-reconnect is the only one of the three that's clearly safe; drop the other two
- Also fix logging order bug in handlePathUpdate that prints 'wifi -> wifi' (lastPrimaryInterface gets assigned before reason string is built)
close_reason: Reverted audio-time-stretch / http-continuous (kept http-reconnect) and fixed path-monitor log order. Build green, tests pass. Held push pending user sign-off.

title: Remove CallKit to clear App Store China rejection
description:
Apple rejected submission 0e6c447f (May 11 2026) under Guideline 5 because the app includes CallKit functionality and lists China as an available territory. MIIT now prohibits CallKit usage in apps on the China App Store.

Current usage is diagnostic-only: a single CXCallObserver in AdagioStream/Services/AudioPlayerService.swift used to (a) log phone-call state changes for CarPlay-interruption debugging and (b) append a calls=[...] field to logAudioSessionSnapshot() output. It is not load-bearing — phone-call pause/resume on CarPlay is driven entirely by AVAudioSession.interruptionNotification, which iOS posts whether or not the app links CallKit.

Scope:
- Remove 'import CallKit', CXCallObserver, callDelegate property, setDelegate call, calls=[...] field in logAudioSessionSnapshot, and the CallObserverDelegate class.
- Leave the DebugLogger .call category in place (unused but harmless; cheap to keep for log-grep compatibility).
- Bump CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION in project.yml, regenerate, build, run on iPhone 17 Pro sim, and verify play/stop still works.

Diagnostic loss: ability to distinguish phone-call interruption vs Siri/long-Siri interruption from the debug log alone. Not used in any production logic.

title: Fold AdagioStreamCore back into AdagioStream-iOS
description: Reverse the bgc.* refactor that split Core into a sibling SwiftPM package. Xcode Cloud only clones the primary repo and the local-path SPM dep '../AdagioStreamCore' has no remote, so CI cannot resolve it. Folding Core back removes the cross-repo dependency entirely. Done when: Core directory is deleted, iOS builds and tests pass without the package, CI script no longer references the sibling.

title: Fix M3U export filename: ShareLink uses raw String, producing 'text-XXXX.data' instead of '<playlist>.m3u'
description: When exporting a custom playlist via the ShareLink in CustomPlaylistDetailView, the receiving app (e.g. Files) names the file something like 'text-4660-A643-31-0.data'. Cause: ShareLink is given the raw M3U string (M3UExporter.export(playlist)) instead of a URL. iOS has no filename for a String, so it invents one. Fix: use M3UExporter.exportToFile(playlist) which already writes a temp '<sanitized-name>.m3u' file, and pass that URL to ShareLink. CustomPlaylistDetailView.swift:61.

title: Reorganize Settings Tab for Better UX
description: Consolidate the current 10-section Settings tab into 5 well-organized sections: Appearance, Playback, Channels & Accounts, Advanced, and About. Goal is to reduce clutter, separate casual vs power-user settings, and improve discoverability.

title: Per-provider stream ID stripping + EPG-based ESPN fuzzy matching
description: Two features: (1) Per-provider toggle to strip numeric stream ID prefix from channel names at load time. (2) EPG-based fuzzy matching so ESPN scores show on any sports channel whose EPG title mentions both teams.

title: Fix ESPN/SXM matching for channels with stream ID prefix in name
description: NHL/NFL/NBA channels from certain XC providers have stream IDs prepended to the name (e.g. '5204 | Radio: Anaheim Ducks'). The prefix stripping logic only handles 'Radio: ' and 'TV: ', so the stream ID prefix prevents matching against ESPN team names and SXM station names.

title: First-Time Setup / Onboarding Flow
description: Welcome screen on first launch that asks for connection type (Xtreme Codes or M3U), name, URL, and credentials. Option to skip for custom M3U users.

title: Fix pull-to-refresh on empty/error channel states
description: Empty/error states in ChannelListView use non-scrollable VStack/EmptyStateView. SwiftUI .refreshable requires scrollable content to detect the pull gesture. Same bug as Android version.

title: CarPlay: Show channel name briefly on channel change
description: When changing channels via steering wheel or CarPlay UI, temporarily display the channel name for 2 seconds before showing track title/artist info

title: Add Custom Playlist support to CarPlay interface
description: Custom playlists should appear in the CarPlay root screen so users can browse and play their custom M3U entries while driving

title: Replace sidebar navigation with universal TabView
description: Remove NavigationSplitView iPad layout and use TabView on all device sizes. iPad Mini is the minimum standard for fit.

title: Regenerate Xcode project and verify iPad build
description: Run xcodegen generate after all project.yml changes. Build for iPad simulator to verify compilation. Ensure Xcode Cloud ci_scripts are compatible (already confirmed device-agnostic). Bump CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION per versioning policy.

title: Fix all deprecated onChange(of:) calls for iOS 17+
description: Migrate 7 deprecated onChange(of:) single-closure calls to the iOS 17+ two-parameter variant across 4 files: ContentView.swift (2), SettingsView.swift (3), RetryableAsyncImage.swift (1), AdagioStreamApp.swift (1). Use .onChange(of: value) { oldValue, newValue in } syntax.

title: Set TARGETED_DEVICE_FAMILY to iPhone and iPad
description: Change TARGETED_DEVICE_FAMILY from '1' (iPhone only) to '1,2' (iPhone + iPad) in project.yml base settings. This is the core change that enables iPad builds. The pbxproj currently has inconsistent values across configurations — xcodegen generate will unify them.

title: Update MPNowPlayingInfoCenter with track metadata
description: When track metadata is available, update MPNowPlayingInfoCenter: MPMediaItemPropertyTitle = track title, MPMediaItemPropertyArtist = joined artist names, MPMediaItemPropertyAlbumTitle = album name. Load Spotify album artwork as MPMediaItemArtwork. Fallback to channel name/group when no track data. This affects Lock Screen, Control Center, and AirPlay displays. Depends on 13.4.

title: Display track metadata in NowPlayingView
description: When a SiriusXM channel is playing and track metadata is available: show song title and artist(s) below the channel name. Display Spotify album artwork instead of (or alongside) the channel logo — use spotifyImageURL large size. Show album name if available. Fallback gracefully to current behavior (channel name + group) when no track data. Depends on 13.4 (polling must be working).

title: Add track metadata polling during SiriusXM playback
description: When AudioPlayerService starts playing a SiriusXM channel, start polling /api/station/{channel} every ~30-60 seconds for the current track. Publish the current TrackMetadata via a @Published property on AudioPlayerService (or a dedicated TrackMetadataService). Stop polling when playback stops or switches to a non-SiriusXM channel. Handle errors gracefully (network failures should not interrupt playback). Depends on 13.2 and 13.3.

title: Implement SiriusXM channel detection and station matching
description: Detect SiriusXM channels by checking if channel.name or channel.group contains 'SiriusXM' (case-insensitive). On first detection, fetch /api/station to build a mapping table. Match local channels to xmplaylist station IDs using fuzzy name matching and/or station number extraction from channel names. Cache the mapping in memory. Add an isSiriusXM computed property or helper. Depends on 13.2 (XMPlaylistService).

title: Build XMPlaylistService API client
description: New service class to talk to xmplaylist.com API. Endpoints needed: GET /api/station (list all stations), GET /api/station/{channel} (recent tracks for a channel). Must set User-Agent header on all requests. Respect rate limits via response headers. Use URLSession with async/await. Parse station list into a local lookup structure (id, name, number). Parse track responses into TrackMetadata model.

title: Create TrackMetadata model
description: New model struct to hold real-time track information: title (String), artists ([String]), albumName (String?), spotifyImageURL (URL?) for album art, spotifyTrackID (String?), timestamp (Date). Conforming to Codable, Identifiable, Hashable. This is the shared currency type all views will consume.

title: Create adaptive dimension utilities
description: Build AdaptiveLayout.swift with size-class-aware constants: channel icons (40->56pt), artwork (200->300+pt), buttons, spacing. Use @ScaledMetric where appropriate for Dynamic Type support. Provide a clean API like AdaptiveLayout.channelIconSize that returns the right value based on horizontal size class.

title: Add NavigationSplitView for channel browsing
description: On iPad, show channel groups in sidebar column and channels in the detail column using NavigationSplitView two-column layout. Keep collapsible sections on iPhone. Depends on 9.1 (adaptive root view must exist first).
close_reason: Channel groups already display well as collapsible sections in the detail pane of the NavigationSplitView. A three-column split would be excessive.

title: Create adaptive root view with sidebar/tab switching
description: Rewrite ContentView.swift to use @Environment(\.horizontalSizeClass). Regular width (iPad): NavigationSplitView with sidebar containing Channels, Favorites, Providers, Settings sections. Compact width (iPhone): keep existing TabView. Both paths share the same underlying views.

title: Enable iPadOS multitasking and multi-scene
description: Set UIApplicationSupportsMultipleScenes = true in project.yml. Verify singleton services (AudioPlayerService, ProviderManager) work correctly across multiple windows. Test Split View, Slide Over, and Stage Manager scenarios. Depends on 8.2 (orientations must be enabled first).

title: Enable all orientations and remove UIRequiresFullScreen
description: Set UIRequiresFullScreen = false in project.yml. Add landscape orientations for iPhone (portrait + landscape left/right) and all orientations for iPad. Ensure Info.plist UISupportedInterfaceOrientations is correct for both device families.

title: Raise deployment target to iOS 17+
description: Deployment target is already iOS 17.0 in project.yml — verify it is correct and consistent. The onChange fixes have been broken out into mobilemusic-3w3.5.

title: Phase 9: Adaptive Navigation Architecture
description: The core structural change: sidebar + NavigationSplitView on iPad, tab bar on iPhone. Rewrite ContentView for adaptive root navigation, add two-column channel browsing, and adapt sheet presentations.

title: Phase 8: iPadOS Foundation
description: Configuration changes that unlock iPad support. Raise deployment target to iOS 17+, enable all orientations, remove UIRequiresFullScreen, and enable iPadOS multitasking and multi-scene support.

title: Move provider credentials to iOS Keychain
Build provider management views (add/edit/delete providers)
Implement MPNowPlayingInfoCenter and MPRemoteCommandCenter integration
Implement ProviderManager (orchestrate loading from any provider type)
Implement XtreamCodesAPI client (async/await REST client)
Configure background audio entitlement and audio session in Info.plist
Implement EPGParser (XMLTV parser using Foundation XMLParser)
Implement AudioPlayerService (AVPlayer, AVAudioSession, buffering)
Implement M3UParser (line-by-line M3U/M3U8 playlist parser)
Phase 4: Audio Playback
Phase 2: Provider & Parsing
Implement AppSettings defaults and initial settings view
Implement PersistenceService (JSON file storage with actor isolation)
Create data models (Channel, Provider, EPGEntry, ChannelGroup, AppSettings)
Initialize Xcode project (SwiftUI App lifecycle, iOS 16+)
Phase 1: Foundation
Cleanup: remove assertSessionOwnership workarounds
Bump CodeQL runner to macos-26 / Xcode 26 for iOS 26 SDK
description: Project uses iOS 26 Liquid Glass APIs (glassEffect, GlassEffectContainer) gated by #available(iOS 26, *). Compiling requires the iOS 26 SDK which only ships with Xcode 26. macos-15 default Xcode 16 fails. Switch runner to macos-26 and pin Xcode 26.5 to match local dev environment.

title: Pin CodeQL runner to macos-15 + Xcode 16 for project format 77
description: First CodeQL fix used macos-14, which defaults to Xcode 15.4. xcodegen emits project format 77 (Xcode 16+), so xcodebuild failed with 'future Xcode project file format' error. Bump runner to macos-15 and explicitly select an Xcode 16.x app.

title: Fix CodeQL Swift autobuild failure for XcodeGen project
description: GitHub's CodeQL default-setup autobuild fails on XcodeGen-based Swift projects because .xcodeproj is generated, not committed. Switched to advanced setup and replaced the auto-generated workflow with one that runs xcodegen generate before xcodebuild. Applied to both main and dev so future merges don't conflict.

title: Relicense repo under AGPL-3.0 with Apple App Store exception
description: Add LICENSE file with AGPL-3.0 text and a §7 Additional Permission for Apple App Store distribution. Update README License section. Copyright holder: End of Line Technologies.

title: MPRemoteCommandCenter targets not effective after CarPlay cold launch
description:
Symptom: after the 16:47:16 CarPlay cold launch, no REMOTE category log entries fired between 16:47:25 (CarPlay-side channel select) and 16:51:46 (first NEXT_TRACK that landed) — a 4m 21s window. Pre-crash session was firing REMOTE commands fluently (10+ in seconds during channel mashing).

Either:
a) MPRemoteCommandCenter.shared() targets weren't registered on the cold-launch path
b) Targets were registered to the wrong AudioPlayerService instance (singleton drift?)
c) The remote events were arriving but ignored because some 'ready' guard wasn't satisfied yet
d) iOS itself wasn't routing remote events to the app until something else happened around 16:51:46

Investigate:
1. Grep for MPRemoteCommandCenter setup in AudioPlayerService — confirm it runs in init() not in some lifecycle hook that doesn't fire on CarPlay-only cold launches.
2. Add a log line at the moment each target is registered (and confirm it ran).
3. Add a log when a remote command is RECEIVED but rejected by any guard (the current REMOTE logs only fire on accepted commands).

What happened at 16:51:46 that made it work? At that timestamp the user pressed NEXT_TRACK on the steering wheel and Alt2K started playing. Look for what changed in the AudioPlayerService state right before that — likely some lazy initialization that finally ran.
close_reason:
Added AudioPlayerService.prewarmRemoteCommands() and call it from CarPlaySceneDelegate.didConnect.

What it does:
1. Logs current audio session category/mode/route, current MPNowPlayingInfoCenter state, and playbackState at CarPlay connect — so the next incident log shows what iOS thinks of us before user action.
2. If nowPlayingInfo is nil, writes a placeholder (rate=0, isLiveStream=true) and sets playbackState=.stopped. The placeholder declares us as a now-playing candidate so iOS routes MPRemoteCommandCenter events to us BEFORE the user picks a channel. Doesn't stomp an active playback session (guards on existingInfo == nil).
3. Re-asserts session.setActive(true) so any stale post-interruption state is cleared.

Build 142; 52 tests pass. Hypothesis confirmation pending real-world data: if the 4-minute REMOTE silence repeats with prewarmRemoteCommands logging in place, we'll see whether the session was active, whether anyone else had nowPlayingInfo, and whether our placeholder write succeeded.

title: Now-Playing metadata not delivered on first channel after CarPlay cold launch
description:
Symptom: after CarPlay cold-launch, the user selected Alt Nation at 16:47:25. SXMMetadataService logged 'Now playing: "Turn" by The Wombats' at 16:47:25.686. CarPlayTemplateManager logged 'pushNowPlaying: pushing new template' at 16:47:25.222. But the user reported no title/artist appeared anywhere.

Investigate:
1. Order of operations: pushNowPlaying happened BEFORE SXM matched the track (.222 vs .686). Verify that subsequent SXM updates trigger a NowPlaying template refresh, not just the initial push.
2. MPNowPlayingInfoCenter wiring: confirm updateNowPlayingInfo() is called when streamTitle/streamArtist change post-cold-launch. The warm path triggers this via Published property observers — verify cold-launched instance has equivalent observation.
3. Add instrumentation: log the payload of every pushNowPlaying call (title, artist, artwork present?) and every MPNowPlayingInfoCenter.default().nowPlayingInfo assignment. Currently we only log that the push happened, not what was in it.

Reproduce by force-killing the app while CarPlay is connected (Settings → General → CarPlay → reboot the head unit, or via Xcode debugger), then reconnecting CarPlay and selecting a channel.
close_reason: Defensive measures shipped (root cause needs real-world data): (1) CarPlayTemplateManager schedules NowPlaying refreshes at t+1s/+3s/+5s with logged attempts. (2) refreshNowPlayingInfo clears ALL change-detection caches to force a fresh write. Build 142; 52 tests pass.

title: CarPlay cold-launch path leaves Now-Playing and remote commands non-functional
description:
Observed symptoms after the FRONTBOARD watchdog kill on 2026-05-20 (separate epic v47):
- After CarPlay cold-launched the app at 16:47:16, the first user-selected channel took ~22s to play (VLC buffering timeout fallback at 20s, then reduced-buffer retry at 3s caches succeeded).
- Once playing, no title/artist appeared on the CarPlay or lock-screen Now-Playing surface, even though SXMMetadataService logged 'Now playing: "Turn" by The Wombats' correctly. The CarPlayTemplateManager logged 'pushNowPlaying: pushing new template' but the log doesn't capture payload contents.
- Steering-wheel channel up/down did nothing for 4m 21s. REMOTE log entries are silent from 16:47:25 (CarPlay channel select) through 16:51:46 (first NEXT_TRACK that landed). After 16:51:46, remote commands worked normally.

Hypothesis: the CarPlay-only cold-launch path doesn't fully wire up MPNowPlayingInfoCenter and MPRemoteCommandCenter targets in the same order/timing as the warm path. Provider recovery (commit 7effe2e) and debug-logger boot (commit cf39dee) already cover other parts of this path; metadata and remote command center are still gaps.

This is the user-visible aftermath of v47 (the crash). Fixing v47 reduces incidence; these still need fixing because cold launches happen for other reasons too (memory eviction, OS reboot, fresh install).
close_reason: All 3 children closed. Build 142 ships defensive measures + instrumentation for the cold-launch CarPlay symptoms. Real-world reproduction with the new logs in place is the next step.

title: Recovery handles empty providers list (still loading at 15s)
description:
scheduleProviderRecoveryCheck (CarPlayTemplateManager.swift:122-155) at the 15s mark filters providerManager.providers.filter(\.isEnabled). If providers is still empty at that moment (e.g. Keychain or persistence is slow on a cold launch), enabled is empty, missing is empty, and the function exits with 'all enabled providers loaded — no retry needed' even though nothing has loaded.

Fix options to consider:
- If providers.isEmpty at the check, reschedule one more time at a longer delay (e.g. 30s) rather than declaring success.
- OR: kick the recovery off providerManager.$providers (combine with $channels) so it only counts as 'loaded' once we have BOTH a non-empty providers list AND non-zero counts per provider.

Done when:
- A CarPlay-only cold launch with slow keychain still triggers a recovery loadChannels() call once providers materialize.
- The 'all loaded' log path requires at least one enabled provider in providers, not just empty == empty.
close_reason: scheduleProviderRecoveryCheck now distinguishes 'provider list still empty' from 'all loaded, all happy'. Before: empty providers array produced empty 'missing' list which took the 'no retry needed' early return. After: if providers.isEmpty at the 15s check, increment a separate emptyProviderListChecks counter (cap 1) and reschedule at +30s. Doesn't burn the providerRecoveryAttempts budget (cap 2) since no reload was triggered. After the reschedule cap, logs 'giving up — no persisted providers?' Build 143; 52 tests pass.

title: Build, install, and verify in simulator
description: Build for iPhone 17 Pro simulator. Install and launch. Run AdagioStreamTests. Verify logging shows up correctly in debug logs by enabling DebugLogger and triggering a refresh.

title: Add CarPlay-side recovery: refresh channels if list is empty after delay
description: In CarPlaySceneDelegate.didConnect, after templateManager.configure(), kick off a recovery task: if visibleChannels remains empty for N seconds AND providers list is non-empty, trigger ProviderManager.shared.loadChannels() again. Catches the case where the cold-launch network race ate the first attempt.
close_reason: Backfill close — shipped in commit 7effe2e (2026-05-15). scheduleProviderRecoveryCheck at CarPlayTemplateManager.swift:127. Improved by 068ed5a (pid.3) tonight. Stranded open when parent epic 3xs was closed.

title: Add per-provider retry to handle background-launch transient failures
description: XCs 3 sequential calls + URLSession defaults fail more often during cold CarPlay background launches. The inner fetch() retries only 5xx/decoding errors, not network errors. Wrap each provider's loadChannels(from:) in a retry loop (e.g. 2 attempts with backoff) for both M3U and XC, since both rely on network.
close_reason: Backfill close — shipped in commit 7effe2e (2026-05-15). loadChannelsWithRetry at ProviderManager.swift:299. Stranded open when parent epic 3xs was closed.

title: Add instrumentation to ProviderManager.loadChannels failure path
description: Currently provider loading errors are swallowed silently into errors[] and only surfaced via self.error (iOS UI only). CarPlay never sees them. Add DebugLogger calls in ProviderManager.loadChannels and loadChannels(from:) to diagnose XC failures.

title: Add NWPathMonitor-driven player reconnect on Wi-Fi <-> cellular path transitions
description:
Currently AudioPlayerService has no network-path monitoring (grep NWPathMonitor returns nothing). VLC's built-in http-reconnect handles connection drops but not network interface changes — when a user walks out of Wi-Fi range onto cellular (or vice versa), the player can stall and not recover.

Implementation sketch:
- Add NWPathMonitor in AudioPlayerService, start on init, stop on deinit
- On path transition (status .satisfied after .unsatisfied, OR isExpensive flips, OR primary interface changes), if currentChannel is set and player state is .error/.stopped/.buffering > N seconds, trigger a fresh player rebuild + retune of the current channel
- Reuse the existing retirePlayer(options:) + startStream() flow so we don't duplicate the tear-down logic
- Log path transitions to the .player category

Avoid: reacting to every transient path event (debounce/cooldown so we don't thrash); reconnecting when playback is healthy
close_reason: Added NWPathMonitor in AudioPlayerService. Init-time monitor logs initial path state; subsequent updates trigger a player rebuild via play(channel:) when status flips unsatisfied->satisfied or the primary interface changes (Wi-Fi <-> cellular). 5s cooldown debounces rapid handoffs. play(channel:)'s same-channel guard is bypassed by clearing isActiveSession first. Build green, 52/52 tests pass, simulator launches. Real-world verification requires enabling Diagnostics logging and toggling airplane mode / Wi-Fi off during playback.

title: Add http-reconnect / http-continuous / audio-time-stretch VLC instance options for cellular resilience
description:
Add three libvlc options at instance creation in AudioPlayerService.startStream():
- --http-reconnect: auto-reconnect on HTTP-level drops
- --http-continuous: continue reading on transient HTTP errors instead of failing the playback
- --audio-time-stretch: allow ±2% rate adjustment to recover from buffer drift instead of dropping samples

Must be set at VLCMediaPlayer init time (alongside --network-caching), NOT via media.addOptions — libvlc rejects those as unsafe via libvlc_media_add_option (see comment at AudioPlayerService.swift:599-601). Only applies to the live-stream path (line ~605); the buffered-file playback path at line ~803 plays a local file and does not need HTTP options.
close_reason: Added --http-reconnect, --http-continuous, --audio-time-stretch to VLCMediaPlayer instance options in AudioPlayerService.startStream() (AudioPlayerService.swift:608-614). Build succeeded, 52/52 unit tests pass, app launches in simulator. Follow-up beads_mobilemusic-81n filed for NWPathMonitor work.

title: Regenerate, build, run tests, delete Core dir
description: xcodegen generate. xcodebuild build + test. Fix any compile errors. Delete the AdagioStreamCore directory.

title: Drop 'import AdagioStreamCore' from all iOS sources
description: Remove the import line from all .swift files in AdagioStream/ that have it. Don't touch other imports.

title: Update project.yml and ci_post_clone.sh
description: Remove AdagioStreamCore from packages: in project.yml. Remove the AdagioStreamCore dependency from AdagioStream and ShareExtension targets. Drop the sibling-clone warning block from ci_scripts/ci_post_clone.sh.

title: Move Core sources and tests into iOS app and test target
description: Move Sources/AdagioStreamCore/{Models,Services,ViewModels,Constants.swift,Extensions.swift} into AdagioStream/{Models,Services,ViewModels,Constants.swift,Utilities/Extensions.swift}. Move Tests/AdagioStreamCoreTests/* (excluding the empty stub) into AdagioStreamTests/Services/. Discard AdagioStreamCore.swift and AdagioStreamCoreTests.swift stubs. Don't change file contents — public keywords stay; #if os(iOS) gates stay.

title: Show 'groups disabled' alert from ProviderListView, not just ProviderManagementView; correct nav path in message
description: When a provider is added via ProviderListView (the standalone Accounts screen), the newProviderInfo alert never fires because only ProviderManagementView observes it. Result: user sees 0 channels and no explanation. Fix: add the same alert modifier to ProviderListView. While there, fix the message to point at the actual path 'Settings → Channels & Accounts → Groups' instead of 'Settings → Groups'.

title: Clean up About section and remove orphaned code
description: Keep About section as-is (App name, Version, Build, Licenses). Remove all orphaned section code from the old layout. Verify no settings were lost in the reorganization.

title: Create Advanced sub-view for power-user settings
description: Create a new AdvancedSettingsView accessible via NavigationLink from the main settings. Move niche/power-user settings here: Channel Sort Order, Group Sort Order, Sort Prefixes (inline, not its own section), Debug Logging toggle + Share/Clear logs, Clear All Favorites (destructive, with confirmation).

title: Create Channels and Accounts section
description: Restructure the overloaded Channels section into a focused Channels and Accounts section. Contains: Accounts (nav link, listed first), Groups (nav link), Grouping mode picker, Reload Channels button. Show Loaded Channels and Favorites counts as subtle footer/inline details.

title: Restructure Playback section with Startup Channel
description: Consolidate playback-related settings into one section. Move Startup Channel here from its own section. Contains: Startup Channel picker, Buffer Duration slider, Stream Quality (read-only), Live Score Updates interval.

title: Merge Appearance, Text Size, and Artwork into single Appearance section
description: Combine the current Appearance, Text Size, and Artwork sections into one Appearance section. Contains: Appearance Mode (System/Light/Dark), Text Size picker, Artwork Display Mode (Cover Art/Channel Logo).

title: Add stripStreamIDs toggle to Provider model and UI
description: Add per-provider opt-in toggle, strip at channel load time, revert always-on regex from ESPN/SXM services

title: Add EPG-based fuzzy matching for ESPN scores
description: Match ESPN games to channels by searching currently-airing EPG titles for both team names

title: Show EPG data as fallback in Now Playing info when ESPN/xmplaylist have no data
description: When neither ESPN API nor xmplaylist API return data for the current channel, fall back to showing the currently-airing EPG entry from the upstream XC connection in the iOS Now Playing Info Center (lock screen, CarPlay, Control Center).

title: Fix crash with duplicate providers in Favorites
description: Duplicate providers produce channels with same IDs, crashing SwiftUI ForEach. Deduplicate at load time.

title: Add AirPlay route picker to Now Playing view
description: Add AVRoutePickerView wrapper and place in Now Playing toolbar

title: Integrate onboarding into ContentView
description: Show onboarding fullScreenCover on first launch

title: Create WelcomeSetupView
description: Multi-step onboarding: welcome, connection type, credentials, skip option

title: Add hasCompletedSetup flag to AppSettings
description: Track first-time setup completion

title: Add AdagioStreamWidget target to project.yml
description: The AdagioStreamWidget/ directory exists with AdagioStreamWidgetBundle.swift and NowPlayingLiveActivity.swift but has no corresponding target in project.yml. It is orphaned from the build system. Add a proper widget extension target with TARGETED_DEVICE_FAMILY '1,2' and correct dependencies.

title: iOS password save dialog triggers on provider credentials
description: SecureField + TextField('Username') pattern in AddProviderView.swift triggered iOS credential detection. Fixed by replacing SecureField with TextField and setting empty textContentType.

title: Settings tab slow: Picker renders 34K channel views eagerly
description: The Startup Channel Picker in SettingsView.swift built Text views for all 34K channels on every render. Fixed by limiting picker to favorites only.

title: App launch slow: 34K channels from Infinity provider
description: The Infinity provider returns 11MB / 34,034 channels on every launch with no caching. This makes the app feel very slow at startup. Need to investigate if channel data was previously cached or if the large provider needs lazy loading.

title: Channel skip does not wrap around at end of list
description: When reaching the last channel in a list (favorites, group, etc.), playNext() silently does nothing instead of wrapping to the first channel. playPrevious() also doesn't wrap at the beginning. Fixed with modular arithmetic in both methods.

title: Race condition: double loadChannels() corrupts enabledGroups
description: ProviderManager.init() and ChannelListView.task both call loadChannels() concurrently. This caused enabledGroups.json to get corrupted to an empty array, which filters out all channels showing 'No Channels'. Fixed with isLoadingChannels guard and empty-set protection in loadEnabledGroups().

title: Reorderable favorites with drag-to-reorder
description: Switch favorites storage from unordered Set<String> to ordered [String] array. Add Edit button to FavoritesView enabling drag-to-reorder and swipe-to-delete. Includes migration from legacy Set storage format.

title: Update Live Activity and CarPlay with track metadata
description: Extend NowPlayingAttributes.ContentState to include optional trackTitle and artistName fields. When track metadata updates, push new state to the Live Activity. In CarPlay (CarPlayTemplateManager), update the now playing template with track info when available. Depends on 13.4.

title: Display track metadata in MiniPlayerView
description: When track metadata is available for a SiriusXM channel, show artist — song title in the mini player subtitle area instead of the channel group. Keep it concise for the small form factor. Depends on 13.4.

title: Phase 13: SiriusXM Track Metadata via xmplaylist
description: Integrate with the free xmplaylist.com API to display real-time song/artist information for SiriusXM channels. Detect SiriusXM channels by group/channel name, match them to xmplaylist station IDs, poll for current track data, and surface it in NowPlayingView, MiniPlayerView, MPNowPlayingInfoCenter, Live Activity, and CarPlay.

title: Add keyboard shortcuts for playback and navigation
description: Space = play/pause, Cmd+1-4 = switch sections (Channels/Favorites/Providers/Settings), Cmd+F = search, Cmd+N/Cmd+P = next/prev channel, Esc = dismiss sheets/modals. Use .keyboardShortcut() modifier and @FocusState for focus management.

title: Make ChannelRowView and channel lists adaptive
description: Larger icons on iPad (use AdaptiveLayout dimensions), show more detail per row. Consider LazyVGrid layout on iPad landscape for a grid of channels instead of a single-column list.

title: Make MiniPlayerView adaptive
description: Larger channel logo on iPad, show more detail (group name, EPG program info) in regular width. Remove hardcoded 49px tab bar padding and calculate dynamically. Ensure mini player looks good at all iPad widths including Split View.

title: Make NowPlayingView adaptive
description: Horizontal layout on iPad landscape (artwork left, controls right). Scale artwork to 300-400pt on iPad. Increase control spacing and button sizes. Use horizontalSizeClass to switch between compact (vertical stack) and regular (horizontal split) layouts.

title: Adapt sheet presentations for iPad
description: Use .presentationDetents for AddProviderView and NowPlayingView sheets. Consider using popovers instead of sheets where appropriate on iPad (e.g., provider add could be a popover from toolbar). Depends on 9.1.

title: Phase 11: Keyboard & Pointer Support
description: iPad with Magic Keyboard and trackpad support. Add keyboard shortcuts for playback/navigation, pointer hover effects, and context menus for right-click.

title: Phase 10: Adaptive Layouts & Responsive Dimensions
description: Replace hardcoded sizes with adaptive values. Create dimension utilities, make NowPlayingView/MiniPlayerView/ChannelRowView/EPGView adaptive, and constrain form widths on iPad.

title: Disable XXE in EPG XMLParser
Add file protection entitlement
Validate URL schemes in M3U parser
Show channel icons alongside names in CarPlay channel lists
description: Channel lists in the CarPlay interface should display the channel's icon/logo next to the channel name, similar to how they appear in the iPhone UI. For example: [ICON] Radio: Sirius XM Chill. Use the channel's logoURL to fetch and display the image in CPListItem.

title: Auto-resume playback after Siri voice dictation ends
description: When the user invokes Siri for voice commands (e.g., taking notes via dictation), audio playback is interrupted but does not automatically resume once Siri/dictation finishes. The app should detect when the audio session interruption ends and resume playback automatically.

title: Stop playback when CarPlay disconnects
description: When the user unplugs from CarPlay, audio playback continues on the iPhone. The app should detect the CarPlay session ending and automatically stop playback, since the user likely intended to stop listening when they disconnected.

title: CarPlay session causes all groups to expand on iPhone
description: When the CarPlay interface is active and the user then opens the app on their iPhone, all channel groups are expanded. This likely happens because CarPlay navigation triggers state changes that propagate to the iPhone UI. Investigate why CarPlay interaction expands groups and prevent CarPlay state from affecting the iPhone group collapse/expand state.

title: Add favorite channels from CarPlay now playing interface
description: Users should be able to favorite/unfavorite channels directly from the CarPlay playback interface without needing to go back to the channel list. Add a favorite button to the CarPlay now playing screen.

title: Dynamic background color for channel icons
description: Channel icons currently display on a white background, which causes white-on-white issues for light-colored icons. The app should analyze the PNG image and pick an appropriate background color that provides good contrast, so icons are always clearly visible.

title: Implement CarPlayTemplateManager (tab bar, lists, now playing templates)
Implement CarPlaySceneDelegate (CPTemplateApplicationSceneDelegate)
Configure CarPlay scene manifest and entitlements
Phase 6: CarPlay Integration
Wire channel selection to playback
Build NowPlayingView and MiniPlayerView
Build EPGView for full schedule display
Implement favorites toggle and FavoritesView
Wire EPG data to channel views (current/next program display)
Build channel list views (grouped by category, search bar)
Phase 5: EPG Integration
Build ChannelListViewModel with search, grouping, and filtering
Phase 3: Channel Browsing
Update README: bump Xcode requirement to 26.0+
description: README listed Xcode 16.3+ but project uses iOS 26 Liquid Glass APIs (glassEffect, GlassEffectContainer). iOS deployment target stays at 17 because the calls are #available-gated, but the SDK to compile them ships only with Xcode 26+.

title: Log full Now-Playing payload (not just 'pushing template')
description:
Current state: CarPlayTemplateManager:337 logs 'pushNowPlaying: pushing new template' with no payload contents. When debugging the cold-launch metadata gap (sibling task on this epic), this log is useless — we can't tell from the log alone whether the template was pushed with empty fields or with valid data that the system then dropped.

Change: extend the log line at CarPlayTemplateManager.swift line ~337 to include title, artist, presence of artwork (boolean), and channel name. Same for any updateNowPlayingInfo() call — dump the relevant keys of the dict being assigned to MPNowPlayingInfoCenter.

Small change, high diagnostic payoff.
close_reason:
Added payload logging at all three NowPlaying surfaces:
1. updateNowPlayingInfo: each write to MPNowPlayingInfoCenter now logs source (which branch chose the title — sxm/espn/streamMetadata/epg/channelNameOverlay/fallback-loading/fallback-channel), title, artist, album, isLive, state name, rate, hasArtwork.
2. clearNowPlayingInfo: logs when nowPlayingInfo is wiped.
3. CarPlayTemplateManager.pushNowPlaying: extended all three log paths (already-on-top, popping back, pushing new) with nowPlayingContext() — current channel name and a live snapshot of MPNowPlayingInfoCenter title/artist/hasArtwork at push time.

Build 142; 52 tests pass.

title: Stage Manager window sizing and toolbar
description: Handle all Stage Manager sizes (1/4 to full screen). Add now-playing toolbar item showing current channel. Add .defaultSize() modifier in AdagioStreamApp WindowGroup. Test minimum window size constraints.

title: Add drag and drop support
description: Drag channels to favorites list. Add Transferable conformance to Channel model. Support dropping M3U URLs to add providers. Use .draggable() and .dropDestination() modifiers.

title: Add context menus for right-click
description: Channel rows: Play, Toggle Favorite, View EPG, Copy Stream URL. Provider rows: Edit, Delete, Reload Channels. Use .contextMenu modifier. These also enable long-press menus on iPhone as a bonus.
close_reason: Context menus already exist on all interactive list items (ChannelRowView, ProviderManagementView, group headers, saved songs, playlists). SwiftUI .contextMenu natively supports right-click on iPadOS.

title: Add pointer hover effects
description: Add .hoverEffect(.highlight) on channel rows, buttons, sidebar items, and other interactive elements. Ensures Magic Keyboard trackpad users get visual feedback when hovering over tappable elements.

title: Constrain form widths on iPad
description: Cap SettingsView and AddProviderView form widths at ~600pt so they don't stretch full-width on iPad. Use .frame(maxWidth:) or .containerRelativeFrame() to center forms on wider screens.
close_reason: SwiftUI Form within NavigationSplitView detail pane and sheet presentations already constrain form widths appropriately on iPad.

title: Make EPGView adaptive for wider screens
description: Use extra iPad width for a timeline/grid view showing multiple hours of EPG data side by side. On compact width, keep the existing vertical list. Consider a horizontal scroll timeline similar to traditional TV guide apps.
close_reason: EPGView uses standard List layout with no hardcoded widths — already adapts to wider screens.

title: Phase 12: iPad Polish & Optimization
description: Final touches for iPad experience. Drag and drop support, Stage Manager window sizing, launch screen scaling, and optional @Observable migration for iOS 17+.

title: Build Settings view (buffer duration picker, provider management)
Add error handling and loading states throughout the app
Phase 7: Polish & Error Handling
Migrate to @Observable (iOS 17+ optimization)
description: Optional optimization. Migrate all ObservableObject view models to @Observable macro. Replace @StateObject with @State, @EnvironmentObject with @Environment. This reduces unnecessary view re-renders and simplifies the observation model. Only possible after iOS 17+ deployment target is set in Phase 8.
close_reason: Deferred — @Observable cannot be applied to NSObject subclasses (AudioPlayerService, TimeShiftBufferService). The remaining services use singletons with .shared which work well with ObservableObject. Risk of regressions outweighs the optimization benefit.

title: Launch screen and visual polish for iPad
description: Scale LaunchScreenView and EmptyStateView dimensions for iPad. Verify glass/blur effects look correct at all iPad sizes. Test all visual elements in Split View, Slide Over, and Stage Manager configurations.

title: Create app icon and launch screen
